title: gh-141786: Fix missing parent executor during JIT trace
author: caje731
body:
An executor's trace can point to another executor, forming a graph of traces / executors. Sometimes it is possible while recording a trace that the parent executor is freed / invalidated halfway. This leads to the bug described in the issue gh-141786, which if left unfixed could cause a memory leak.

This patch checks for the validity of the parent executor as well as allowing JIT from the cold executor. While the cold executor is not linked, it is the executor responsible for creating side traces and we still want to JIT from it.

comments.author: caje731
comments.body: @Fidget-Spinner who the issue is currently assigned to has helped me with this PR.

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: @devdanzin can we use your repro as a test case please?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Sure, in this case and any others, whenever you want.

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: @caje731 please add a test case to `Lib/test/test_capi/test_opt.py` by copying the script from here https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141786#issuecomment-3572014739. You can take a look at `test_interpreter_finalization_with_generator_alive` to see how to run a test in a subprocess easily.

comments.author: caje731
comments.body:
I should note that the following simple diff in the test:
 ```
 diff --git a/Lib/test/test_capi/test_opt.py b/Lib/test/test_capi/test_opt.py
index 0f67f19a115..9027be79254 100644
--- a/Lib/test/test_capi/test_opt.py
+++ b/Lib/test/test_capi/test_opt.py
@@ -2772,7 +2772,7 @@ def B(B):
                 if rand.random() < 0.1:
                     C(B)
 
-            for D in range(200):
+            for D in range(350):
                 try:
                     B(D)
                 except:
```

produces a breakage in the test:
```
...
stderr:
---
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 136, in <module>
    b()
    ~^^
  File "<string>", line 101, in b
    del B
        ^
UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'B' where it is not associated with a value
---
```

At this point I'm unsure if this is related to JIT optimizations, but superficially seems to be just a side-effect of the symbol-conflicts (ab)used in the test.

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: @markshannon i would like to merge this still. Even with your proposed fix it's possible to invalidate an executor while tracing. This is a separate issue.
